dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: SeaTunnelX One-stop Operation and Maintenance Management Overview
dashboard.welcomeDesc: Start by adding hosts and deploying your first SeaTunnel cluster

logout.success: You have been logged out successfully

callback.title: Welcome to SeaTunnel Operations Platform
callback.footer: SeaTunnel Operations Platform - Making data integration easier

terms.agreement: By logging in, you agree to our

termsDialog.title: Terms of Service
termsDialog.description: Please read the following terms of service carefully. By using this service, you agree to these terms.

general.title: 1. General Terms
general.content1: These Terms of Service (hereinafter referred to as "Terms") set forth the conditions for your use of the SeaTunnel Operations Platform.
general.content2: By accessing or using our services, you agree to be bound by these terms. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our services.
general.content3: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Modified terms will take effect immediately upon posting.

usage.title: 2. Usage Rules
usage.content1: You agree to use our services in a lawful and responsible manner, strictly complying with applicable laws and regulations.
usage.content2: Prohibited behaviors include but are not limited to:
usage.prohibited1: Posting illegal, harmful, threatening, abusive, harassing, defamatory, or otherwise objectionable content
usage.prohibited2: Attempting unauthorized access to our systems or other users' accounts
usage.prohibited3: Interfering with or disrupting the normal operation of services
usage.prohibited4: Violating any applicable laws or regulations

content.title: 3. Content Standards
content.intro: To maintain a healthy online environment, distribution of the following types of content is strictly prohibited:
content.pornography: Pornographic content: Any content containing pornography, obscenity, nudity, or sexual suggestions
content.promotion: Promotional content: Commercial advertisements, marketing promotions, spam, or other commercial content
content.illegal: Illegal content: Any content that violates applicable laws and regulations
content.harmful: Harmful information: Violence, terrorism, extremism, or content that endangers national security
content.false: False information: Rumors, fake news, or misleading information
content.infringement: Infringing content: Content that infringes on intellectual property rights, privacy rights, or other legal rights of others
content.warning: Once violating content is discovered, we will immediately remove it and may terminate the associated account.

legal.title: 4. Legal Compliance
legal.intro: This service strictly complies with applicable laws and regulations, including but not limited to:
legal.law5: Network Information Content Ecological Governance Regulations
legal.compliance: Users must comply with the above laws and regulations and other relevant provisions when using the service.
legal.cooperation: For violations of laws and regulations, we will cooperate with relevant authorities for investigation and handling.

account.title: 5. Account Responsibility
account.content1: You are responsible for maintaining the accuracy and security of your account information.
account.content2: You are responsible for all activities conducted using your account.
account.content3: If you discover any unauthorized use, please notify us immediately.

intellectual.title: 6. Intellectual Property
intellectual.content1: All content in the service, including text, graphics, logos, images, and software, is protected by copyright and other intellectual property laws.
intellectual.content2: Without our explicit written permission, you may not copy, modify, distribute, or otherwise use this content.

limitation.title: 7. Limitation of Liability
limitation.content1: To the maximum extent permitted by law, we are not liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages.
limitation.content2: Our total liability shall not exceed the amount you paid for the service in the past 12 months.

privacyDialog.title: Privacy Policy
privacyDialog.description: We value your privacy. This policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your personal information.

collection.title: 1. Information Collection
collection.intro: We collect the following types of information:
collection.item1: Account information: Public information such as username and avatar obtained through OAuth
collection.item2: Usage data: Information about how you use our services
collection.item3: Technical information: IP address, device information, browser type, etc.
collection.item4: Log information: Server logs and error reports

usage.title: 2. Information Usage
usage.intro: We use the collected information for:

sharing.title: 3. Information Sharing
sharing.content1: We do not sell, exchange, or rent your personal information to third parties.
sharing.content2: We may share your information in the following circumstances:
sharing.item3: To protect our rights and property
sharing.item4: With trusted service providers (subject to confidentiality agreements)

security.title: 4. Data Security
security.intro: We employ industry-standard security measures to protect your information:
security.item3: Regular security audits and vulnerability scanning
security.warning: Please note that no data transmission or storage method is 100% secure.

retention.title: 5. Data Retention
retention.intro: We retain your personal information only for as long as necessary:
retention.item1: Account information: Duration of account existence
retention.deletion: You can request deletion of your account and related data at any time.

rights.title: 6. Your Rights
rights.intro: You have the following rights regarding your personal information:
rights.item1: Right of access: View information we hold about you
rights.item2: Right of correction: Correct inaccurate information
rights.item3: Right of deletion: Request deletion of your personal information
rights.item4: Right to restrict processing: Limit how we process your information

profile.title: Profile
profile.invalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address
profile.description: SeaTunnelX is a one-stop operations platform for data integration, providing fast, secure, and convenient data synchronization services. The platform supports multiple data sources with comprehensive task management and monitoring capabilities.

host.title: Host Management
host.description: Manage hosts for SeaTunnel cluster deployment
host.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete host {name}? This action cannot be undone.
host.name: Host Name
host.k8sCredentialsHint: Provide either kubeconfig or token for authentication
host.leaveEmptyToKeep: Leave empty to keep current value
host.uninstallCommandTip: Add --remove-logs to also remove log files

errors.dockerUrlInvalid: Invalid Docker API URL format (use tcp:// or unix://)
errors.k8sUrlInvalid: Invalid Kubernetes API URL format (use https:// or http://)

cluster.title: Cluster Management
cluster.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete cluster {name}? This action cannot be undone.
cluster.deleteConfirmWarning: After deletion, the cluster and node records will be removed from the control plane, and agents on each host will be notified to stop processes. If "Force delete" is checked, agents will also be told to remove the install directory on the host (e.g. /opt/seatunnel-xxx); data cannot be recovered.
cluster.forceDeleteOption: Force delete (also remove install directory on hosts)
cluster.name: Cluster Name
cluster.deploymentModeCannotChange: Deployment mode cannot be changed after creation
cluster.hybridDescription: Master and worker run on the same nodes
cluster.separatedDescription: Master and worker run on different nodes
cluster.nodeInstallDirDescription: SeaTunnel installation directory on this node
cluster.startSuccessAutoRestart: Start request submitted, auto-restart will manage the startup process
cluster.restartSuccessAutoRestart: Restart request submitted, auto-restart will manage the startup process
cluster.startConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to start cluster {name}?
cluster.stopConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to stop cluster {name}? All running tasks will be interrupted.
cluster.restartConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to restart cluster {name}? All running tasks will be interrupted.
cluster.nodeStartConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to start node {name}?
cluster.nodeStopConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to stop node {name}?
cluster.nodeRestartConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to restart node {name}?
cluster.batchStartConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to start {count} selected node(s)?
cluster.batchStopConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to stop {count} selected node(s)?
cluster.batchRestartConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to restart {count} selected node(s)?
cluster.addNodeDescription: Add a host as a node to this cluster
cluster.removeNodeConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove node {name} from this cluster?
cluster.noAvailableHosts: No available hosts with Agent installed
cluster.onlyAgentInstalledHosts: Only hosts with Agent installed can be added as nodes
cluster.workerRoleDescription: Worker node executes data synchronization tasks
cluster.selectAtLeastOneRole: Select at least one node role
cluster.hybridRoleFixedDescription: In hybrid mode the role is fixed to Master/Worker, so each node carries both responsibilities.
cluster.separatedRoleSelectionDescription: In separated mode, one host can create both Master and Worker logical nodes.
cluster.sameHostCreatesTwoNodes: This will create two logical node instances on the same host, each with its own ports and JVM override.
cluster.roleConfigDescription: Ports and JVM override settings for the {role} role
cluster.portConfigDescription: Ports will be validated via Agent when adding the node
cluster.jvmOverrideEnabledHint: This node will use its custom JVM heap setting first.
cluster.jvmInheritDefault: Override is disabled. Current inherited cluster default: {value} GB
cluster.jvmNoClusterDefault: No cluster JVM default is configured yet. Enable override to set a dedicated value for this node.
cluster.jvmHeapSizeHint: Only the current role's heap override is stored. Other roles still inherit cluster defaults.
cluster.jvmHeapSizeRequired: Heap size must be greater than 0 when JVM override is enabled
cluster.selectNodesToOperate: Please select nodes from the list below to operate
cluster.nodeStartSuccessAutoRestart: Start request submitted, auto-restart will manage the startup process
cluster.nodeRestartSuccessAutoRestart: Restart request submitted, auto-restart will manage the startup process
cluster.batchStartSuccessAutoRestart: Batch start request submitted, auto-restart will manage the startup process
cluster.batchRestartSuccessAutoRestart: Batch restart request submitted, auto-restart will manage the startup process

wizard.title: Deploy Cluster Wizard
wizard.basicDesc: Configure cluster name and deployment mode
wizard.precheckDesc: Check if all selected hosts meet the installation requirements
wizard.selectHostsDesc: Select hosts with Agent installed to deploy SeaTunnel
wizard.separatedRoleHint: A node can be both Master and Worker (deployment still runs two separate processes)
wizard.noAvailableHostsDesc: Please add hosts with Agent installed first
wizard.selectPluginsDesc: Select plugins to install with SeaTunnel (optional)
wizard.pluginsOptional: Plugin selection is optional. The system can prepare selected plugins during installation.
wizard.deployWarning: Please review the configuration carefully. Deployment will install SeaTunnel on all selected hosts.
wizard.deployingDesc: Installing SeaTunnel on selected hosts. This may take a few minutes.
wizard.deployCompleteDesc: Your SeaTunnel cluster has been deployed successfully.
wizard.confirmCancel: Deployment is in progress. Are you sure you want to cancel?
wizard.noLocalPackagesDesc: Please go to Package Management to download or upload packages before using offline installation mode.
wizard.packageAlreadyDownloaded: This version package is already downloaded locally
wizard.packageWillDownload: Package will be downloaded automatically during deployment
wizard.packageWillDownloadDesc: Online mode will automatically download the package from the mirror source. Once downloaded, it will be saved locally for future use.
wizard.selectLocalPluginsDesc: Select locally downloaded plugins to install (optional)
wizard.noLocalPluginsForVersion: No local plugins for this version
wizard.noLocalPluginsForVersionDesc: No local plugins downloaded for version {version}. Please go to Plugin Marketplace to download the required plugins first.
wizard.pluginsPreparedAutomatically: If no local cache is available, the system will prepare selected plugins and dependencies during installation.
wizard.packageNotFoundDesc: Package for version {version} is not available locally. It will be downloaded from the mirror source during deployment.
wizard.mirrorHint: If the package is not available locally, it will be downloaded from the selected mirror source during deployment.
wizard.manualUploadDesc: If you cannot access the network, you can manually upload the package.
wizard.workerPortDesc: Worker node port for separated mode, default 5802
wizard.readyToPrecheckDesc: Click the button below to check if {count} hosts meet the installation requirements
wizard.precheckFailedWarning: Some hosts failed precheck. Please resolve the issues and recheck.
wizard.precheckPassedInfo: All hosts passed precheck. Ready to deploy.

plugin.marketplaceDesc: Browse and manage SeaTunnel plugins and dependencies

plugin.selectClusterToViewInstalled: Select a cluster to view installed plugins
plugin.goToClusterDetail: Go to cluster detail page to manage installed plugins
plugin.catalogSourceHint: Loading and refreshing plugins always use the official Maven repository; downloads use the currently selected mirror.
plugin.searchPlaceholder: Search plugins by name or description
plugin.loadingFromMavenDesc: First load may take 30-60 seconds, subsequent loads will use cache
plugin.loadedFromMaven: Plugin list reloaded from Maven repository
plugin.loadedFromMavenDesc: This request missed the backend cache; subsequent requests for the same version should use cache
plugin.versionNote: Connector version matches the SeaTunnel main version
plugin.transformBuiltInDesc: All Transform connectors are included in seatunnel-transforms-v2.jar, shipped with SeaTunnel installation package. No separate download needed, enabled by default.
plugin.uploadCustomPlugin: Upload custom developed connector JAR files
plugin.customPluginNote: Custom plugin connector JARs go to the connectors directory, and dependencies are placed in lib or plugins directories based on the target rule
plugin.customComingSoon: This feature is not available yet. Coming soon.
plugin.restartClusterAfterInstallPrompt: This connector includes lib-directory dependencies. After installing to cluster "{cluster}", a cluster restart is usually required to take effect.
plugin.restartClusterAfterInstallSuccess: Restart request for cluster "{cluster}" has been submitted
plugin.restartClusterOnNextStartHint: This connector includes lib-directory dependencies and will take effect the next time cluster "{cluster}" starts.
plugin.versionMismatchClusterHint: Cluster version is v{clusterVersion}, but this plugin package is v{pluginVersion}
plugin.versionMismatchInstalledHint: Cluster version is v{clusterVersion}, and the installed plugin version is v{installedVersion}
plugin.uninstallConfirm: Are you sure you want to uninstall plugin {name}? This will remove the plugin from all nodes in the cluster.
plugin.installPluginDesc: Select a target cluster to install this plugin
plugin.managePluginDesc: Install this plugin to clusters and auto-attach the required connectors and dependencies.
plugin.noClustersAvailable: No clusters available. Please create a cluster first.
plugin.installNote: The plugin will be installed on all nodes of the selected cluster. Connector JARs will be placed in the connectors directory, and dependencies will be placed in the lib or plugins directories.
plugin.downloadStarted: Download queued, check progress in the Local Plugins tab
plugin.downloadAllStarted: Submitted {count} plugins to the download queue, check progress in the Local Plugins tab
plugin.downloadAllSuccess: Download tasks submitted: {total} total, {downloaded} new, {skipped} skipped (already exist)
plugin.downloadAllProfileDialogTitle: Confirm profile dependencies for batch download
plugin.downloadAllProfileDialogDescription: The following plugins have multiple selectable profiles. All profiles are preselected by default; review them before continuing.
plugin.downloadAllProfileEmpty: No selectable profiles are available right now.
plugin.downloadAllProfilePluginHint: These dependencies will be downloaded together with the plugin package.
plugin.localPluginsDesc: Plugin packages downloaded to Control Plane local storage
plugin.downloadedDesc: Plugin packages downloaded to Control Plane local storage and ready to install to clusters
plugin.downloadFromAvailable: Download plugins from the 'Available Plugins' tab
plugin.name: Plugin Name
plugin.deleteConfirmDesc: Are you sure you want to delete local plugin {name}? You will need to download it again to install to clusters.
plugin.batchInstallDesc: Install {count} connectors to the selected cluster
plugin.allClustersInstalled: This connector is already installed on all available clusters
plugin.dependenciesDesc: Configure dependencies required by the connector (e.g., database drivers), will be downloaded with the connector
plugin.versionPlaceholder: Leave empty to use connector version
plugin.allFieldsRequired: Group ID, Artifact ID and Version are all required
plugin.deleteDependencyConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete dependency {name}?
plugin.noConfiguredDependencies: No configured dependencies, add them in "Configure Dependencies"
plugin.parseMavenXmlDesc: Paste Maven dependency XML to auto-parse groupId, artifactId and version
plugin.mavenXmlParseError: Parse failed, please check XML format
plugin.officialDependenciesDesc: The system automatically attaches the required connectors and dependencies during install. Only add custom dependencies when the standard template does not cover your case.
plugin.officialDependenciesUnavailable: Automatic install information is not available yet.
plugin.officialDependenciesNotRequired: This plugin does not require extra dependencies for installation.
plugin.officialDependenciesUnknown: No standard dependency template matched yet. Add custom dependencies if needed.
plugin.configuredDependenciesDesc: Use this section to add your own extra dependencies. It does not overwrite the system template.
plugin.installPathIsolatedDesc: For SeaTunnel 2.3.12+, plugin-specific dependencies are placed under plugins/<mapping>, while shared dependencies can still stay in lib/.
plugin.installPathLegacyDesc: For this version, dependencies are installed under lib/ by default.
plugin.selectProfilesToPreview: Select one or more scenarios to preview the automatic install content.
plugin.automaticInstallContentDesc: The system installs companion connectors and extra dependencies based on the standard template.
plugin.noAttachedConnectors: No extra connectors need to be attached.
plugin.extraDependenciesDesc: These dependencies will be installed into their target directories automatically.
plugin.installNoteDynamic: Connector JARs will be placed in {connectorDir}; {dependencyMessage}
plugin.installNoteDependencies: Extra dependencies will be placed in {dirs}.
plugin.installNoteNoDependencies: No extra dependencies are required for this install.
plugin.selectScenarioFirst: Please select the JDBC scenario(s) first.

dependencyStatusDesc.ready: The system will automatically attach {count} install item(s).
dependencyStatusDesc.profileRequired: Select the data source scenario before downloading and installing.
dependencyStatusDesc.notRequired: This plugin does not require extra dependencies for installation.
dependencyStatusDesc.unknown: No standard dependency template matched yet. Please review before install.

plugin.uploadJarHint: Use this when the system template does not fit your case, such as patched or private JARs. Artifact / Version will be inferred from the file name when possible.

plugin.uploadArtifactIdPlaceholder: Optional, inferred from file name by default
plugin.uploadVersionPlaceholder: Optional, inferred from file name by default
plugin.activeOfficialDependenciesDesc: If you prefer your own version, disable the system dependency first and then add your custom dependency.
plugin.disabledOfficialDependenciesDesc: These system dependencies will be excluded from future downloads and installs.

installer.installDirDesc: SeaTunnel will be installed to this directory, default includes version number for multi-version coexistence

installer.onlineVersionsDesc: SeaTunnel versions available for download from mirrors
installer.confirmDeletePackage: Are you sure you want to delete package version {version}?

installer.pleaseSelectFileAndVersion: Please select a file and enter version

installer.agentRequiredForInstall: Agent must be installed and online before installing SeaTunnel.

userManagement.title: User Management
userManagement.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete user {username}? This action cannot be undone.
userManagement.passwordPlaceholder: Leave empty to keep current password

errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters
errors.usernameTooShort: Username must be at least 3 characters
errors.invalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address
errors.cannotDeleteSelf: Cannot delete current logged in user

assistant.title: SeaTunnel Assistant
assistant.description: Having issues? Paste your error logs or describe your problem below. The assistant will help locate issues based on SeaTunnel source code.
assistant.privacyNotice: To protect your privacy, the system automatically filters sensitive information like IP addresses, passwords, and API keys. Please review the preview before sending.
assistant.inputLabel: Enter your question or error log
assistant.inputPlaceholder: Paste error logs, exception stack traces, or describe your issue...
assistant.openDeepWikiTip: Content copied, paste it in the search box at the bottom of DeepWiki page

monitor.title: Monitor Configuration
monitor.description: Configure process monitoring and auto-restart settings
monitor.descriptionWithCluster: Configure process monitoring and auto-restart settings for cluster {name}
monitor.autoMonitorDesc: Continuously monitor node and process health while recording runtime events. This is always on and cannot be disabled.
monitor.monitorIntervalHint: Check process status every N seconds (1-60)
monitor.maxRestartsHint: Maximum restart attempts in time window (1-10)
monitor.timeWindowHint: Time window for counting restarts (60-3600 seconds)
monitor.cooldownPeriodHint: Wait time after reaching max restarts (60-86400 seconds)

monitoringCenter.title: Monitoring Center
monitoringCenter.subtitle: Unified workspace for monitoring, alert handling, and notifications.

alerts.title: Alert Center
alerts.subtitle: Unified view of local process alerts and remote Alertmanager alerts
alerts.stateHint: “Firing / Resolved” describes whether the alert itself is still active. “Pending / Acknowledged / Silenced” describes human handling progress. Acknowledged does not mean resolved.

actionHints.silenceReady: Temporarily mute this still-firing alert for 30 minutes.
actionHints.silenceUnavailable: Only firing alerts that are not already silenced can be silenced.
actionHints.closeReady: Manually close this incident and stop follow-up notifications for the same incident.
actionHints.closeClosed: This alert has already been manually closed.

rules.title: Alert Rules
rules.subtitle: Configure rule switches, thresholds and windows per cluster
rules.selectClusterHint: Select a cluster to view and edit rules

policyCenter.title: Alert Policy Center
policyCenter.subtitle: Unify platform-health and Prometheus-backed policies behind one capability-aware product surface.
policyCenter.unifiedExperienceHint: User-facing alerting stays unified in one policy center, while the backend continues to separate platform-health signals from Prometheus-based signals and only exposes advanced builders when the required integrations are ready.
policyCenter.loadError: Failed to load alert policy center bootstrap
policyCenter.channelListLoadError: Failed to load notification channels for policy binding
policyCenter.clusterListLoadError: Failed to load clusters for policy scope selection
policyCenter.templatesSectionSubtitle: These templates define the product skeleton of the unified policy center: platform-health templates always remain evolvable, while metrics templates unlock when Prometheus capabilities are available.
policyCenter.builderSubtitle: Persist unified alert policies that bind source templates, scope, severity, cooldown, and notification channels into one resource.
policyCenter.empty: No alert policies yet. Create the first unified policy from the builder on the right.
policyCenter.formInvalid: Please provide a policy name and a valid template or PromQL expression.
policyCenter.cooldownInvalid: Cooldown must be a valid non-negative integer.
policyCenter.toggleError: Failed to update alert policy state
policyCenter.enabledHint: The policy is persisted immediately but only evaluated when enabled.
policyCenter.sendRecoveryHint: Send a recovery / resolved notification when the alert clears.
policyCenter.channelsHint: Select the notification channels that should receive this policy when it fires.
policyCenter.noChannelsAvailable: No notification channels available yet. You can still create the policy now and bind channels later.
policyCenter.builderReadyHint: This builder is available and ready to create persisted alert policy resources.
policyCenter.builderUnavailableHint: This builder is currently unavailable because required integrations are not ready.
policyCenter.legacyRulesDescription: The existing cluster-scoped runtime rules remain active and serve as the first compatibility layer while the unified policy resource model is being introduced.
policyCenter.legacyRulesCardSubtitle: Keep using the current cluster-scoped runtime rule editor as a transition surface while unified alert policies are being introduced.

placeholders.name: For example: Production masters unavailable
placeholders.description: Describe the operational meaning and intended responders of this policy.

platform_health.title: Platform Health Policies
platform_health.summary: Build productized policies from SeaTunnelX-managed cluster health, node liveness, process stability, and operation outcomes.

metrics_templates.title: Metrics Templates
metrics_templates.summary: When Prometheus is ready, unlock curated templates for CPU, memory, FD, failed jobs, and other metrics-based policies.

custom_promql.title: Custom PromQL
custom_promql.summary: Give advanced users a custom PromQL entry point while preserving a unified policy and notification experience.

remote_ingest.title: Remote Alert Ingest
remote_ingest.summary: Bring Alertmanager webhook alerts into the same alert center and delivery pipeline.

webhook_notification.title: Webhook / IM Notifications
webhook_notification.summary: Deliver alert notifications through Webhook, WeCom, DingTalk, Feishu, and similar webhook-compatible channels.

in_app_notification.title: In-App Notification Center
in_app_notification.summary: Provide a built-in inbox, recovery follow-up, and receiver-facing experience directly inside SeaTunnelX.

platform_health.title: Platform Health Policies
platform_health.description: Prioritize productized policies for master availability, worker sufficiency, node offline, agent offline, and restart failures.

metrics_template.title: Metrics Template Policies
metrics_template.description: Once Prometheus and metrics targets are ready, start with curated templates for CPU, memory, FD, failed jobs, and more.

custom_promql.title: Custom PromQL
custom_promql.description: Open a custom PromQL builder for advanced cases beyond the curated templates.

master_unavailable.name: Master unavailable
master_unavailable.description: Fire when the cluster no longer has a healthy master / coordinator.

worker_insufficient.name: Healthy workers below threshold
worker_insufficient.description: Fire when the healthy worker count falls below the configured baseline.

node_offline.name: Node offline
node_offline.description: Fire when node heartbeat or runtime visibility is lost for a sustained duration.

agent_offline.name: Agent offline
agent_offline.description: Fire when the control plane loses the agent connection needed for operations.

process_crashed.name: Process crashed
process_crashed.description: Track repeated crashes of managed SeaTunnel processes.

process_restart_failed.name: Process restart failed
process_restart_failed.description: Fire when automatic restart can no longer recover the failed process.

process_restart_limit_reached.name: Restart limit reached
process_restart_limit_reached.description: Detect crash-loop behavior after restart limits are exceeded.

cluster_restart_requested.name: Restart requested
cluster_restart_requested.description: Fire when a user triggers a cluster restart or node restart from the control plane, useful for delivery validation and change awareness.

node_stop_requested.name: Node stop requested
node_stop_requested.description: Fire immediately when a user manually stops one node from the control plane so operators can track explicit stop actions.

cpu_usage_high.name: CPU usage high
cpu_usage_high.description: Use Prometheus metrics to detect sustained CPU saturation.

memory_usage_high.name: Memory usage high
memory_usage_high.description: Use Prometheus metrics to catch memory pressure before node instability escalates.

fd_usage_high.name: FD usage high
fd_usage_high.description: Use the metrics stack to detect file-descriptor exhaustion risk.

failed_jobs_high.name: Failed jobs high
failed_jobs_high.description: Use Prometheus metrics to detect abnormal failed-job or retry volumes.

split_brain_risk.name: Split-brain risk
split_brain_risk.description: Use Hazelcast cluster-safety metrics to detect unsafe-cluster or split-brain risk.

job_thread_pool_queue_backlog_high.name: Job thread pool queue backlog high
job_thread_pool_queue_backlog_high.description: Alert when the SeaTunnel job execution queue keeps piling up before latency and starvation spread.

job_thread_pool_rejection_high.name: Job thread pool rejections detected
job_thread_pool_rejection_high.description: Alert when the job thread pool starts rejecting work instead of queuing or executing it.

deadlocked_threads_detected.name: Deadlocked threads detected
deadlocked_threads_detected.description: Detect JVM deadlocks that can stall task scheduling, execution, and recovery.

policyCenter.editSubtitle: Update an existing unified alert policy without leaving the unified policy center.
policyCenter.editingHint: You are editing policy "{name}". Changes here will overwrite the persisted policy resource.
policyCenter.editModeHint: Edit mode keeps the existing policy resource and writes changes back in place.

history.title: Execution History · {name}
history.subtitle: Latest policy-linked delivery attempts. Total {total} records · generated at {generatedAt}.
history.loadError: Failed to load alert policy execution history
history.empty: No delivery history for this policy yet.

bridgeHints.legacyRuntimeRule: This template currently executes through the existing cluster runtime rule path, so it must target one concrete cluster during the transition period.
bridgeHints.resourceOnly: This policy currently persists in the unified policy domain only. Its full evaluation engine will be connected in a later slice.
bridgeHints.directChannelDispatch: Because this policy already binds notification channels, critical local process events can now trigger direct unified policy notifications.
bridgeHints.noDirectDispatch: Bind one or more notification channels here to enable direct unified policy notification dispatch.

phase3.title: Notification Channels
phase3.subtitle: Manage alert notification channels, test sends and notification routes (Webhook / Email / WeCom / DingTalk / Feishu).
phase3.channelFormInvalid: Please provide channel name and endpoint
phase3.routeFormInvalid: Please provide route name and select a notification channel

grafana.title: SeaTunnel Monitoring Dashboard
grafana.subtitle: SeaTunnel metrics dashboard.
grafana.notConfigured: Grafana URL is not configured. Please set observability.grafana.url in config.yaml.
grafana.loadFailed: Dashboard loading timed out or failed. Please retry.

platformHealth.title: Platform Health Summary
platformHealth.loadError: Failed to load platform health summary

history.title: Notification History
history.subtitle: Review alert delivery results and failure reasons.

policyCenterV2.title: Alert Policies
policyCenterV2.subtitle: A DorisManager-inspired policy editor that keeps only the controls users actually need.
policyCenterV2.receiverRequired: Select at least one notification recipient, or keep recipients configured on the current email channel
policyCenterV2.concreteClusterRequired: This template must be bound to a specific cluster
policyCenterV2.concreteClusterHint: Runtime event policies must target a concrete cluster instead of all clusters.

placeholders.name: For example: Cluster restart notification
placeholders.description: Add extra notes so operators understand the alert intent quickly.

policyCenterV2.staticConfigDesc: Start from platform-health or metric templates.
policyCenterV2.customPromqlDesc: Write your own PromQL when Prometheus is available.
policyCenterV2.receiverHint: Recipients come from active users with valid emails. Current signed-in user is selected by default when available; otherwise fall back to admins.
policyCenterV2.receiverEmpty: No notifiable users yet. Add a valid email in your profile (or User Management) first.

methods.noEmailConfig: No email config yet, click to create one
methods.noWebhookConfig: No webhook config yet, click to create one

policyCenterV2.recoveryDesc: Send a recovery notification after the alert is cleared.

channel.dialogSubtitle: Configure SMTP delivery for alert emails. Recipients are selected in alert policies, while test send can target one temporary user.

placeholders.recipients: Separate multiple recipients with commas, semicolons, or new lines

channel.testReceiverRequired: Select a test recipient or keep legacy fallback recipients on this email channel
channel.testReceiverCurrentUserMissingEmail: The current signed-in account does not have a valid email yet. Add one in your profile before sending a test email
channel.testRecipientPlaceholder: Choose a temporary recipient for test send
channel.testRecipientNone: Do not pick a recipient yet
channel.testRecipientHint: This only affects test send. Real alert recipients are selected in the policy editor.
channel.testRecipientHintSelected: Test send will be delivered to {value}
channel.testRecipientHintCurrentUserMissing: The current signed-in account "{value}" does not have a valid email yet. Add one in your profile before sending a test email.
channel.legacyRecipientsConfigured: This email channel still has {count} legacy fallback recipient(s) configured for compatibility.
channel.legacyRecipientsEmpty: No legacy fallback recipients are configured. Alert policies should choose recipients directly.

lastTest.idle: No test has been sent in this session yet

connectionLastTest.idle: Connection has not been tested in this session yet

channel.statusOutdated: Current form has changed since the last test result
channel.unsavedChangesConfirm: You have unsaved email channel changes. Discard them?

webhook.dialogSubtitle: Configure outbound webhook / IM channels for unified alerts. The downstream system decides the final recipients; this channel only delivers the alert payload.
webhook.nameRequired: Please enter a webhook config name
webhook.statusOutdated: The form has changed. The test result above may be outdated.
webhook.unsavedChangesConfirm: This webhook config has unsaved changes. Discard them?
webhook.secretHint: Optional. Fill it when the downstream system requires a shared secret or signature key.

placeholders.secret: Optional shared secret or signature key

lastTest.idle: No test send has been executed in this session

policyCenterV2.conditionWindowInvalid: Window must be between 0 and 10080 minutes
policyCenterV2.conditionHint: Keep the template defaults or override the operator, threshold, and evaluation window.
policyCenterV2.customPromqlLegacyDesc: New policies no longer expose custom PromQL. This editor is kept only for existing legacy policies.
policyCenterV2.metricsUnavailableHint: Prometheus metric templates are currently unavailable: {reason}

discovery.title: Auto-Discover Cluster Nodes
discovery.description: Scan host {host} for existing SeaTunnel cluster nodes
discovery.readyToDiscover: Click the button below to scan for SeaTunnel cluster nodes on this host
discovery.noClustersFound: No SeaTunnel cluster nodes found on this host
discovery.selectAtLeastOne: Please select at least one cluster to import
discovery.selectProcessHint: Selecting a node will auto-fill install directory and role
discovery.discoverHint: Select a host with Agent installed, click Auto-Discover to scan running SeaTunnel cluster nodes
discovery.discoverMultiHostHint: Select one or more hosts with Agent installed, click Auto-Discover to scan SeaTunnel cluster nodes on each host

diagnosticsCenter.title: Diagnostics Center
diagnosticsCenter.subtitle: Start from problem clues, run a diagnosis check, and collect live evidence with a diagnostic report when needed.
diagnosticsCenter.comingSoon: {tab} will be implemented in upcoming phases.

tabDescriptions.errors: Aggregate historical anomalies as problem clues and continue investigating from there.
tabDescriptions.inspections: Run a lightweight check first and decide whether live evidence collection is needed.
tabDescriptions.tasks: Review diagnosis execution, bundles, and diagnostic reports.

errors.title: Error Center
errors.globalHint: Group Seatunnel ERROR events by fingerprint for cross-cluster triage.
errors.keywordPlaceholder: Search summary, exception, or source file
errors.empty: No error groups match the current filters.
errors.selectGroup: Select an error group to inspect sampled events and evidence.

inspections.title: Diagnosis Check
inspections.globalHint: Select a managed cluster to run a lightweight diagnosis check first, and then decide whether to collect live evidence.
inspections.clusterScopedHint: Diagnosis checks are filtered for cluster {name}.
inspections.startSuccess: Inspection completed and the latest report is now open.
inspections.lookbackHint: This applies only to the newly started inspection: only evaluate error events and process anomalies within the latest N minutes so already-fixed historical issues are not reintroduced.
inspections.lookbackRangeError: Inspection window must be between 5 and 1440 minutes
inspections.errorThresholdHint: When one error group appears this many times within the inspection window, the inspection creates a recent error burst finding. Default is 1.
inspections.errorThresholdRangeError: Error trigger threshold must be between 1 and 1000
inspections.empty: No inspection reports match the current filters.
inspections.selectReport: Select an inspection report to review its findings.
inspections.summaryFallback: No inspection summary is available for this report.
inspections.noFindings: This inspection produced no structured findings.

detailPage.title: Inspection Detail
detailPage.notFound: Inspection report does not exist or failed to load
detailPage.hasFindingsHint: This inspection found {count} issues. You can generate the diagnostic bundle and report in one click.
detailPage.noBundleNeeded: Inspection completed without issues. No diagnostic bundle is needed.
detailPage.confirmBundleDescription: This collects error logs, alerts, and metric evidence in the selected time window, with optional thread dump and JVM dump.
detailPage.bundleLookbackHint: Defaults to the inspection window. Adjust it if needed to capture evidence from a different time range.
detailPage.includeThreadDumpHint: Useful for analyzing thread states, blocking, and deadlocks.
detailPage.includeJVMDumpHint: Large artifact. Enable only when deeper memory analysis is needed.
detailPage.executionLogsDescription: Diagnostic bundle generation steps and execution status

followUp.title: Continue Diagnosis
followUp.generateHint: Download the bundle and HTML report from this page after generation.
followUp.noFindingsDescription: No abnormal findings require live evidence collection.

autoPolicies.title: Auto Inspection Policies
autoPolicies.description: Configure auto-inspection trigger conditions to start inspections automatically when matched.
autoPolicies.clusterHint: Choose a global policy to apply to every cluster, or select a specific cluster to scope it there only.
autoPolicies.cooldownHint: The same policy will not trigger again during the cooldown window after it fires.
autoPolicies.autoBundleTitle: Auto Generate Diagnostic Bundle and Report
autoPolicies.autoBundleHint: When this policy matches and triggers an inspection, automatically create a diagnostic task to collect thread dumps, JVM dumps, and log samples.
autoPolicies.includeThreadDumpHint: Recommended to stay enabled for analyzing thread states and potential deadlocks.
autoPolicies.includeJVMDumpHint: Recommended only for memory issues such as OOM because dump files can be large.
autoPolicies.logSampleLinesHint: Controls how many lines are sampled for each key step. More lines provide more context but also increase bundle size.
autoPolicies.jvmMinFreeMBLabel: Minimum Free Space for JVM Dump (MB)
autoPolicies.jvmMinFreeMBHint: Applies only when JVM dump collection is enabled to avoid generating dumps on hosts with too little free space.
autoPolicies.autoStartTaskLabel: Automatically start the diagnostic task after creation (when off, the task is only created and not run immediately)

tasks.title: Diagnosis Execution
tasks.globalHint: You can start a diagnosis manually or continue investigating from an error, an inspection finding, or an alert clue.
tasks.clusterScopedHint: Diagnosis records are filtered by cluster {name}, and you can continue investigating around this cluster.
tasks.summaryFallback: This diagnostic task has no summary yet.
tasks.loadDetailError: Failed to load diagnostic task detail
tasks.loadLogsError: Failed to load diagnostic task logs
tasks.chooseClusterFirst: Please select a target cluster first
tasks.selectTask: Select a diagnostic task to inspect real-time steps, node states, and logs.
tasks.empty: No diagnostic tasks match the current filters.
tasks.noSteps: This task has no step records yet.
tasks.noNodes: This task has no node execution records yet.
tasks.noLogs: No execution logs match the current filters.
tasks.includeThreadDumpHint: Recommended by default to help identify blocking threads and deadlocks quickly.
tasks.includeJVMDumpHint: Large artifacts; disk space is verified before collection starts.
tasks.jvmDumpMinFreeMB: Minimum Free Space for JVM Dump (MB)

createContext.manual: This runs a general diagnosis for the selected cluster and is useful when you want a baseline live snapshot first.
createContext.error_group: This continues the investigation around the selected historical error group and automatically links recent affected nodes and error evidence.
createContext.inspection_finding: This continues the investigation around the selected inspection finding and brings the related report and finding automatically.
createContext.alert: This continues the investigation around the selected alert and supplements alert snapshots together with live cluster evidence.

tasks.triggerSourcePlaceholder: Select the trigger source for this diagnostic task
tasks.triggerSourceHint: The system records the task source, and you can decide which basis to use for this diagnostic task here.
tasks.prefillAvailableHint: The problem clue brought from the previous page is still available here. Switch back above if you want to continue diagnosing around it again.
tasks.createModePlaceholder: Choose how to start this diagnosis
tasks.createModeHint: This decides whether the diagnosis starts manually or continues from an existing problem clue.
tasks.manualOnlyHint: There is no error, inspection finding, or alert clue prefilled right now, so only manual start is available. Enter from those pages if you want to diagnose around a specific problem.
tasks.noDiagnosisBasis: No specific problem clue is attached right now, so this diagnosis will collect general evidence for the selected cluster.

stUpgrade.prepareDescription: Choose the target version, run precheck, and confirm that all required assets are ready.
stUpgrade.configDescription: Compare the old-version config with the new-version config, then confirm the final result.
stUpgrade.executeDescription: Run the batch upgrade and observe the step tree, node states, and live logs.
stUpgrade.upgradeInputDescription: It is recommended to prepare packages and plugins before running precheck.
stUpgrade.targetInstallDirDescription: A version-based directory is suggested by default. Override it here if you want the new version installed elsewhere.
stUpgrade.packageChecksumPlaceholder: Leave empty to reuse the checksum from the local package
stUpgrade.connectorNamesPlaceholder: Optional, comma separated; current cluster connectors are reused by default
stUpgrade.continueRequiresLatestPrecheck: Upgrade input has changed. Please rerun precheck before continuing.
stUpgrade.precheckReady: Precheck passed. You can continue to config merge and plan creation.
stUpgrade.precheckUpdated: Precheck result updated. Please resolve blocking issues.
stUpgrade.draftChangedNoticeTitle: Upgrade input has changed, so the current precheck result is outdated.
stUpgrade.draftChangedNoticeDescription: You just changed the target version, target install directory, or other upgrade inputs. The issue list and node target list below are still based on the previous precheck result. Please rerun precheck before continuing.
stUpgrade.precheckSummaryDescription: Shows the current state of the package, plugin assets, config, and node targets.
stUpgrade.issuesDescription: Fix package, connector, and node issues first. Config conflicts can continue to merge page.
stUpgrade.noIssues: No blocking issues at the moment.
stUpgrade.resolveNodeIssues: Back to cluster detail for node issues
stUpgrade.initClusterConfigsSuccess: Cluster configs were initialized. Please review the refreshed precheck result.
stUpgrade.initClusterConfigsUnavailable: No node install directory is available for config initialization.
stUpgrade.nodeTargetsDescription: This upgrade will affect the following nodes and target install directories.
stUpgrade.nodeTargetsPreviewDescription: You changed the upgrade input, so the table below previews the target version and target directory from the current draft. The final result still depends on rerunning precheck.
stUpgrade.loadPrepareFailed: Failed to load the upgrade prepare page
stUpgrade.missingDraft: No upgrade draft found. Please complete the prepare page first.
stUpgrade.mergeGuide: Compare the old-version config and the new-version config line by line. The middle pane is always the final result, and only differing lines show action buttons.
stUpgrade.conflictListDescription: Only differences that require your decision are shown here. Choose between the old-version value and the new-version value, or edit the final result directly.
stUpgrade.noConfigFiles: No config files are available to show.
stUpgrade.noConflicts: There are no pending differences for this file.
stUpgrade.planBlocked: The upgrade plan is still blocked. Please continue resolving issues.
stUpgrade.upgradeCompletedDescription: You can go back to the cluster detail page to review the version, node status, and running state.
stUpgrade.streamFallbackActivated: The event stream failed, so the page switched to polling automatically.
stUpgrade.executionProgressDescription: Shows the completion ratio using the fixed step codes of the upgrade workflow.
stUpgrade.restartExecutionTitle: The previous upgrade task has finished. You can rerun the current plan.
stUpgrade.restartExecutionDescription: A new task will be created from the current upgrade plan, while the previous task steps and logs stay available for comparison and troubleshooting.
stUpgrade.loadExecuteFailed: Failed to load the upgrade execution page
stUpgrade.missingPlan: No upgrade plan found. Please create a plan from the config merge page first.
stUpgrade.stepTreeDescription: Shows the fixed step chain and the execution result of each step.
stUpgrade.nodeExecutionsDescription: Shows the current step, status, and latest message of each node.
stUpgrade.upgradeRecordsDescription: Review historical upgrade tasks for this cluster and open the execution detail of any task.
stUpgrade.logsDescription: Filter persisted logs by step, node, and level.
stUpgrade.noLogs: No logs match the current filters.
stUpgrade.configReadyToPlan: There are no pending differences. You can create the upgrade plan now.
stUpgrade.configReadyToPlanDescription: If the old-version config and the new-version config are identical, there will be nothing to handle here.
stUpgrade.configManualResolutionNotice: There are still unresolved differences, and the system will not decide which side to keep for you.
stUpgrade.configManualResolutionDescription: Resolve each difference by choosing “Use Old Version Value” or “Use New Version Value”, or edit the middle result directly. Plan creation stays blocked until everything is handled.
stUpgrade.mergedResultDescription: The middle pane is the final config that will be applied. Pull content in with the arrows first, then fine-tune it manually if needed.
stUpgrade.conflictResultHint: Edit the middle result directly, or apply the old-version value / new-version value with one click.
stUpgrade.conflictApplied: Applied the selected value into the final result
stUpgrade.resolveConflictsBeforeCreatePlan: Handle all config differences before creating the upgrade plan
stUpgrade.manualMergeResolvedTitle: Differences in this file have been handled.
stUpgrade.manualMergeResolvedDescription: You have already confirmed the final result for this file. You can review the remaining files or create the upgrade plan.
stUpgrade.autoMergeNoChangeTitle: This file has no conflicts and no extra merge work is needed.
stUpgrade.autoMergeNoChangeDescription: The current cluster config and the target version config are already compatible for this file, so no extra merge action is required.
stUpgrade.autoMergeKeepCurrentTitle: This file automatically keeps the current cluster config.
stUpgrade.autoMergeKeepCurrentDescription: The target version does not introduce a new change here, so the system keeps the config that is already effective in the current cluster.
stUpgrade.autoMergeUseTargetTitle: This file automatically uses the target version config.
stUpgrade.autoMergeUseTargetDescription: The current cluster does not have a custom change here, so the system adopts the config shipped with the target version.
stUpgrade.autoMergeCombinedTitle: This file was auto-merged without conflicts.
stUpgrade.autoMergeCombinedDescription: Both sides changed this file, but the changes do not conflict with each other, so the system already produced the final merged result.
stUpgrade.noDifferenceTitle: The old-version config and the new-version config are identical for this file.
stUpgrade.noDifferenceDescription: No extra action is required here. The current result shown on the page can be used directly.
stUpgrade.differenceItemsDescription: These positions still differ. Like a merge editor, use the arrows beside the middle pane to pull in the old or new value, then keep editing the result if needed.
stUpgrade.fileAppliedFromOld: Applied the old-version file into the middle result
stUpgrade.fileAppliedFromNew: Applied the new-version file into the middle result
stUpgrade.removedLineLabel: This line is not kept in the final result
stUpgrade.pluginUpgradeBehaviorDescription: Prepare the target-version plugin package locally before running upgrade. During upgrade, connector JARs switch to the target version, while dependencies under lib and plugins are incrementally overlaid from the locally prepared target-version plugin package. Package-bundled base JARs with the same name are preserved and not replaced.
stUpgrade.upgradeSmokeCheckDescription: After the cluster restarts and becomes healthy, the system runs a template seatunnel.sh job once. If it fails, the result is recorded as a warning only and does not block a successful upgrade.
